module.name: diff
module.spec:
Overview: UnifiedDiff computes a unified-diff style comparison of two text inputs, suitable for human review in `drift diff` output. The format follows the conventional `@@ -oldStart,oldCount +newStart,newCount @@` hunk-header shape with single-character prefixes on body lines.
Defined terms: hunk — a contiguous region of change surrounded by up to contextLines (R4) of unchanged context on each side op — a single classified line; kind is ' ' (context), '-' (removed), or '+' (added) context — an op of kind ' ' change — an op of kind '-' or '+'
Requirements: R1. UnifiedDiff(old, new) MUST return an empty string when old and new are equal (byte-identical after line normalization per R3). R2. UnifiedDiff MUST return an empty string when both inputs are empty. R3. UnifiedDiff MUST normalize "\r\n" to "\n" before comparison so that Windows-style newlines do not produce spurious diffs. R4. Each hunk MUST include up to 3 lines of unchanged context on each side of a change region. Two change regions separated by more than 2*3+1 context lines MUST produce separate hunks. R5. Each hunk body line MUST be prefixed with one of: ' ' (context), '-' (removed from old), '+' (added in new). R6. The hunk header MUST be of the form "@@ -OS,OC +NS,NC @@" where OS/NS are 1-based start line numbers and OC/NC are line counts. When count is 1, the ",N" suffix MUST be omitted (e.g. "@@ -5 +6,2 @@"), matching GNU diff convention. R7. When count is 0, the start MUST be decremented by 1 (e.g. an insertion at line 5 of old reports "@@ -4,0 +5,N @@"), matching GNU diff convention. R8. The output MUST NOT have a trailing newline; callers add line breaks as needed. R9. The diff algorithm MUST be the classic O(m*n) LCS dynamic-programming algorithm. The output is deterministic for equal inputs regardless of platform or runtime.
Algorithm: (i) Split both inputs into lines (splitLines). (ii) Compute the LCS DP table (lcsTable). (iii) Walk the table backwards to classify each line as context/removed/added (diffOps). (iv) Group ops into hunks of up to 3 context lines each side (buildHunks). (v) Format each hunk header + body (formatHunkHeader, formatRange).
See also: cli.diff_command, output.diff_seed_label.

Overview: formatRange formats a single side (old or new) of a hunk header per GNU diff conventions.
Defined terms: count — the number of lines on this side covered by the hunk (may be 0, 1, or many)
Requirements: R1. With count == 0, formatRange MUST emit "prefix(start-1),0" where start is the 1-based line number before the insertion point. (GNU convention: zero-count ranges report the line before.) R2. With count == 1, formatRange MUST emit "prefix start" with no comma or count suffix. R3. With count > 1, formatRange MUST emit "prefix start,count". R4. itoa(n) MUST return the decimal representation of n, including a leading '-' for negative inputs and "0" for zero.
See also: diff.unified_format.
